gate_result: HIGH confidence, 0 anomalies, 0 warnings — gate passed after parser fix

guides.degree_title: Master of Science, Software Engineering - AI Engineering
Master of Science, Software Engineering - Domain Driven Design
Master of Science, Software Engineering - DevOps Engineering
Bachelor of Science, Software Engineering (BSSWE to MSSWE)
guides.medium_detail: SP: 'Scripting and Programming Foundations'; AoS: 'Scripting and Programming - Foundations'. Same course — hyphen variant between SP table label and AoS section heading. 0 AoS anomalies. 38/38 courses present with complete content. Bridge guide covering full BSSWE-to-MSSWE curriculum.

sp_format: All 4 guides use 3-column multiline format (with Term). No 2-column variants in this family.

metadata_format_variants.note: All 4 guides use header-line format. All page_count=0.

capstone_notes.note: No capstone section in any swe_grad guide.

structural_notes: 3 pure graduate guides (MSSWEAIE, MSSWEDDD, MSSWEDOE) are track specializations sharing an identical 10-course structure: [Software]=9, [Risk Management]=1.
MSSWEUG is a bridge guide analogous to MSITUG (graduate_standard) and MSCSUG (cs_grad). Unlike MSCSUG, MSSWEUG has no SP extraction failure — 38/38 rows clean.

Parser fix (_is_bullet_continuation Title Case guard) was required to achieve HIGH for the 3 pure graduate guides. The fix is general and safe — regression-verified against 8 previously-validated guides from multiple families.

MSSWEUG MEDIUM is due to a title hyphenation difference between SP and AoS ('Scripting and Programming Foundations' vs '- Foundations'). Not a parser failure — both titles are present in the source; the SP table uses a slightly different abbreviation than the AoS heading.

parser_changes_made.change: Added Title Case ratio guard: if ≥80% of a line's words start uppercase, return False (not a continuation). Prevents Title Case course titles from being swallowed by bullets that lack terminal punctuation.
parser_changes_made.triggered_by: swe_grad family (consistent SQAD loss across 3 guides)
parser_changes_made.regression_verified: BSCS, BSCSIA, MBA, MHA, MATELED, BSSESB, BSACC, MSCIN — all unchanged

fields_to_exclude: MSSWEUG SP title for 'Scripting and Programming Foundations' — use AoS title ('Scripting and Programming - Foundations') as the canonical form; they refer to the same course

recommendation.summary: 4/4 parsed, 0 failures, 0 LOW. 3 HIGH / 1 MEDIUM. MEDIUM (MSSWEUG) is a cosmetic title variant in a bridge guide — no data loss. Parser fix implemented and verified. Family structure matched gate expectations throughout.
recommendation.downstream_go_nogo: GO — all fields safe. Minor title variant in MSSWEUG noted above.
